title: getPageHtml() XPath branch only returns the first matching node's text
body: In core.ts, when `config.selector` starts with `/` (an XPath), getPageHtml() calls `elements.iterateNext()` once and returns that single result's textContent. If the XPath matches multiple nodes on the page, everything after the first match is silently dropped from the crawled content, unlike the CSS-selector branch which at least targets a single container intentionally.

title: Load resourceExclusions from a file instead of the hardcoded list
body: config.ts's `resourceExclusions` example in the doc comment is an enormous hardcoded array of extensions. It'd be nicer to ship a default JSON/text file of common binary extensions and let users override/extend it, rather than everyone copy-pasting or trimming that giant inline list into their own config.

title: Interactive CLI doesn't explain supported match-pattern glob syntax
body: Running the interactive prompts from cli.ts, the 'match' question just asks for a URL pattern with an example like `https://www.builder.io/c/docs/**`, but doesn't say anywhere (in the prompt or a linked doc) what glob syntax is actually supported (e.g. can you use `*` vs `**`, character classes, etc.).

title: server.ts crawl endpoint doesn't bound maxPagesToCrawl
body: Posting a crawl config to the server.ts API endpoint with an extremely large `maxPagesToCrawl` (or omitting reasonable limits) doesn't seem to be rejected or capped server-side, so a single API call could kick off a crawl of many thousands of pages against someone's site unintentionally (or maliciously).

title: Container run script assumes default Playwright browser cache path
body: containerapp/run.sh appears to assume Playwright's browsers are cached at the default location baked into the base image. Building on top of a custom Docker base image with a different `PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH` breaks the crawl at startup with a browser-not-found error that isn't obvious from the script itself.
